mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) as a game-changer in India's clean energy transition and its role in achieving net-zero emissions.
Discuss the benefits and challenges of India-US collaboration in advanced nuclear technology, particularly concerning regulatory frameworks and technology transfer.
Evaluate how SMRs can contribute to energy security and reduce the carbon footprint compared to conventional energy sources, especially for industrial applications.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized diagram of a Small Modular Reactor power plant.
GS3.trivia: The first commercial SMR is expected to be operational in the US by 2029.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New Support Price Mechanism for Pulses to Boost Domestic Production
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare unveiled a revamped Minimum Support Price (MSP) mechanism specifically for pulses, aiming to incentivize farmers and reduce import dependency.
• The new mechanism includes a dynamic pricing formula linked to input costs and market demand, ensuring better returns for pulse growers.
• It also introduces a procurement guarantee for a certain percentage of the harvest, providing greater price stability and reducing post-harvest losses.
• India is a major consumer and importer of pulses, and this initiative is designed to achieve self-sufficiency in pulse production within the next five years.
• Farmer producer organizations (FPOs) will play a crucial role in the procurement and distribution process, enhancing market linkages for small and marginal farmers.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: New MSP mechanism for pulses announced by Ministry of Agriculture.
Includes dynamic pricing formula and procurement guarantee.
Aims to incentivize farmers and reduce import dependency.
Goal: self-sufficiency in pulse production in five years.
FPOs to play crucial role in procurement and distribution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the need for a revamped MSP mechanism for pulses in India, considering issues of food security, farmer income, and import dependency.
Discuss the potential impacts of a dynamic pricing formula and procurement guarantee on pulse production and market dynamics.
Evaluate the role of FPOs in strengthening the value chain for pulses and ensuring fair returns to small and marginal farmers.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting a field of lentils, symbolizing pulse cultivation.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's largest producer, consumer, and importer of pulses.

GS3.headline: Indigenous mRNA Vaccine for New Variant Shows Promising Results in Phase III Trials
GS3.explainer:
• An indigenous mRNA vaccine, developed by an Indian biotech firm, has demonstrated high efficacy and safety in its Phase III clinical trials against a new, rapidly spreading viral variant.
• The vaccine utilizes cutting-edge mRNA technology, similar to leading global vaccines, but with optimized cold chain requirements suitable for India's diverse climate.
• The trial results, published in a leading medical journal, showed a 92% efficacy rate in preventing severe disease and hospitalization, with minimal side effects.
• This breakthrough marks a significant milestone in India's vaccine development capabilities and its preparedness for future pandemics.
• Regulatory approval is anticipated within months, paving the way for mass production and distribution to bolster public health defenses.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
prelimsPoints: Indigenous mRNA vaccine shows 92% efficacy in Phase III trials.
Effective against new viral variant.
Optimized cold chain for India.
Significant milestone in India's vaccine development.
Regulatory approval expected soon.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous mRNA vaccine development for India's public health security and its role in global pandemic preparedness.
Analyze the advantages of mRNA vaccine technology and the challenges in its development and deployment in a country like India.
Examine how this breakthrough contributes to India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in the healthcare sector and its potential for vaccine diplomacy.
GS3.imageDescription: A scientist holding a vial of vaccine in a laboratory setting.
GS3.trivia: mRNA vaccines work by teaching our cells how to make a protein—or even just a piece of a protein—that triggers an immune response inside our bodies.

GS3.headline: Indian Navy Concludes Multinational Naval Exercise in Indo-Pacific, Enhances Interoperability
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy successfully concluded a major multinational naval exercise, 'Sea Guardian 2025', in the strategically vital Indo-Pacific region, involving navies from 10 partner nations.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, maritime domain awareness, anti-submarine warfare, and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief (HADR) operations.
• It underscored India's commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and its role as a net security provider in the region.
• The drills involved complex maneuvers, live-fire exercises, and joint planning sessions, strengthening professional bonds among participating forces.
• This exercise is part of India's broader strategy to deepen maritime security cooperation with like-minded nations to address common challenges in the Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Indian Navy concluded 'Sea Guardian 2025' multinational exercise.
Held in Indo-Pacific, involved 10 partner nations.
Focus on interoperability, maritime domain awareness, HADR.
Underscores India's commitment to free, open Indo-Pacific.
Part of strategy to deepen maritime security cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of multinational naval exercises like 'Sea Guardian 2025' for India's security interests in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how such exercises contribute to enhancing interoperability and building trust among partner nations in addressing regional maritime challenges.
Evaluate India's role as a 'net security provider' in the Indo-Pacific and the implications of its growing maritime diplomacy.
GS3.imageDescription: Warships from multiple navies sailing in formation on the open sea.
GS3.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's five oceans and is critical for global trade routes.
